children.heading: Credit to Reimbursable Fund or Appropriation.—
children.label: a Credit to Reimbursable Fund or Appropriation.—
A
children.text: not appropriated from the general fund of the Treasury; or

children.label: B
children.text: appropriated from the general fund of the Treasury but by law reimbursable from assessment, tax, or other revenue or receipts.

children.label: A
children.text: credited to the applicable reimbursable fund or appropriation; or

children.label: B
children.text: paid to the federal agency that determined the property to be excess.

children.heading: .—Calculation of net proceeds
children.label: 3 .—Calculation of net proceeds
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the net proceeds of a disposition or transfer of property are the proceeds less all expenses incurred for the disposition or transfer, including care and handling.

children.heading: .—Alternative credit to miscellaneous receipts
children.label: 4 .—Alternative credit to miscellaneous receipts
children.text: If the agency that determined the property to be excess decides that it is uneconomical or impractical to ascertain the amount of net proceeds, the proceeds shall be credited to miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: Special Account for Refunds or Payments for Breach.—
children.label: b Special Account for Refunds or Payments for Breach.—
A
children.text: appropriate refunds to purchasers for dispositions that are rescinded or that do not become final; and

children.heading: .—Withdrawals
children.label: 2 .—Withdrawals
children.text: A federal agency that deposits proceeds in a special account under paragraph (1) may withdraw amounts to be refunded or paid from the account without regard to the origin of the amounts withdrawn.

children.heading: .—Credit to Cost of Contractor’s Work
children.label: c .—Credit to Cost of Contractor’s Work
children.text: If a contract made by an executive agency, or a subcontract under that contract, authorizes the proceeds of a sale of property in the custody of a contractor or subcontractor to be credited to the price or cost of work covered by the contract or subcontract, then the proceeds of the sale shall be credited in accordance with the contract or subcontract.

children.heading: .—Acceptance of Property Instead of Cash
children.label: d .—Acceptance of Property Instead of Cash
children.text: An executive agency entitled to receive cash under a contract for the lease, sale, or other disposition of surplus property may accept property instead of cash if the President determines that the property is strategic or critical material. The property is valued at the prevailing market price when the cash payment becomes due.

children.heading: .—Management of Credit, Leases, and Permits
children.label: e .—Management of Credit, Leases, and Permits

children.text: shall administer and manage the credit, lease, or permit, and any security for the credit, lease, or permit; and
may enforce, adjust, and settle any right of the Government with respect to the credit, lease, or permit.
